description:
A software developer keeps all of her web–project sources in
    /home/user/source_project and wants a **mirrored backup** stored in
    /home/user/backup_server.  
    Your job is to synchronise the two locations with rsync and create a
    plain-text log that documents the transfer.

    INITIAL STATE  
    1. /home/user/source_project already exists and contains exactly three
       regular files organised like this:

          /home/user/source_project/index.html
          /home/user/source_project/script.js
          /home/user/source_project/assets/style.css

       The assets directory already exists; no other files or sub-directories
       are present.
    2. /home/user/backup_server is present but **empty**.
    3. /home/user/rsync_logs exists and is writeable by the normal user.

    REQUIRED END STATE  
    • /home/user/backup_server must be an exact, byte-for-byte copy of
      /home/user/source_project, including the assets sub-directory.
    • A new file called
      /home/user/rsync_logs/initial_sync.log must have been written.
      The file has to be the **raw stdout** produced by rsync when you run it
      with the “verbose” and “stats” flags.
      Two specific lines **must be present somewhere inside the log** (case
      sensitive, no extra spaces):

          sending incremental file list
          Number of files: 4

      Apart from those lines, the rest of the log can be whatever rsync
      normally prints; the tests will only check that the file exists and
      contains those two exact lines.

    CONSTRAINTS  
    • Do NOT delete /home/user/source_project.  
    • Use at most four terminal commands to finish the whole job
      (directory creation, rsync execution, etc.).  
    • The rsync command **must** include the options “-a” and “--stats”.
      (You may add others such as “-v” or “--delete” if you wish.)

    HINTS (not exhaustive)  
    1. Create /home/user/backup_server if it is missing.  
    2. Redirect rsync’s stdout to /home/user/rsync_logs/initial_sync.log  
       (e.g. with “>” or “tee”).  
    3. Verify, then exit.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data the autograder will rely on.
        This information MUST stay secret from the agent.
    -->

    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/source_project/index.html"><![CDATA[<!-- version 1 -->
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_project/script.js"><![CDATA[// version 1
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/source_project/assets/style.css"><![CDATA[/* version 1 */
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <verification>
        <!-- After successful completion -->
        <mirrored_directory>/home/user/backup_server</mirrored_directory>

        <required_log>/home/user/rsync_logs/initial_sync.log</required_log>

        <!-- The grader will check that this file exists and contains BOTH lines -->
        <log_must_contain>
            sending incremental file list
            Number of files: 4
        </log_must_contain>
    </verification>
